summary: A lightweight, extensible Python library that parses JSON configuration files and validates them against JSON Schema definitions, with support for custom validation rules and detailed error reporting. Designed as a reusable module with no external dependencies beyond standard libraries and jsonschema.

components.name: ConfigParser
components.responsibility: Reads and parses JSON files from disk or string input; handles malformed JSON with graceful error reporting.

components.name: SchemaValidator
components.responsibility: Validates parsed configuration against a JSON Schema; aggregates standard and custom validation errors.

components.name: ValidationResult
components.responsibility: Encapsulates validation outcome including is_valid flag and list of ValidationError objects.

components.name: ValidationError
components.responsibility: Represents a single validation failure with path, message, and optional cause.

components.name: CustomRuleRegistry
components.responsibility: Manages registration and execution of user-defined validation functions during schema validation.

deployment_strategy: Distribute as PyPI package; install via pip. No runtime deployment — consumed as importable library.
security_considerations: Input file paths are validated to prevent directory traversal (e.g., pathlib.Path.resolve() with strict checks)
JSON parsing uses standard json.loads() without eval or unsafe deserialization
No execution of arbitrary code from schemas or config files
Custom validation functions are user-provided and assumed trusted (no sandboxing)
scalability_notes: Designed for synchronous, single-config validation; not intended for high-throughput streaming
Memory usage scales linearly with JSON size; large files handled via streaming-aware parsing (not implemented but extensible)
Custom rules must be CPU-efficient to avoid blocking; no built-in async support but compatible with asyncio via wrapping
Extensible architecture allows plugging in alternative validators (e.g., fastjsonschema) without breaking core API
